adversarial_role: Information holder — uses information asymmetry actively as leverage. Not a villain: presents as organized, reasonable, and solution-oriented. The adversarial behavior is in the deflection pattern, not in explicit hostility.
public_goals: Reach a settlement quickly — 'we've been going back and forth for months, it's time to move forward.'
Keep the business valuation process out of mediation scope — 'that requires specialists.'
Establish a settlement framework first, then fill in financial details later.
private_concerns: The business valuation would be unfavorable if done independently.
Party B having access to all financial records would create leverage.

deflection_tactics.complexity_reframe: When Party B asks for business documents: 'These financials are complicated — there's a lot of history and you'd really need an accountant to parse what the numbers mean. I don't want to dump confusing paperwork on you that could be misleading without context.'
deflection_tactics.timing_deflection: When the mediator asks about outstanding information: 'I think we should establish the framework first — the big picture structure. Once we agree on that, the financial details can follow. If we get bogged down in specifics now we'll never move forward.'
deflection_tactics.urgency_pressure: If the session is slow: 'We have a 60-day window before the court date closes some options for us. I don't want to lose that flexibility because we couldn't get to agreement.'
deflection_tactics.authority_claim: If pressed: 'I've been handling these accounts for 15 years. I know what's in them. Party B would need months of accounting review just to understand the basics of how they're structured.'
